body.u: After leaving school at 17 I got a secretarial job in a Sheerness firm, twinlocks I think unless that was later, anyway. I stayed theirtheretilluntil I was 19 then got a job in London in a much bigger firm and better wages. I led quite a good life, had my own car and flat. I

t was about 18 months later that I met my husband. And another year later I married him. For the first 10 months of our marriage we lived in a one bedroomed flat I stayed on at work. Then he changed jobs and we moved to a house nearer my parents.
I left work to sort the house out and get straight.

It was soon after that I fell for our first baby. A girl Lisa, then three years later a boy Stephen. As soon as they got older and Stephen started school I got another part time job locally as a receptionist. But I only stayed 2 years then pack up work altogether. But I didn’t laze around all day, bringing up two young children prevented me anyway. I started at a an art school in the afternoons which I enjoyed.
The rest of my years I enjoyed very much, having two lovely children (not always lovely!) around.
We had a holiday each year, always abroad then when the children left and married, we moved to another, smaller house, locally. We were a very close family and our children regularly visited us.
There was only one bad, really unhappy time which was when my parent died, they both died of old age, no illnesses, my father the first, and my mother broken hearted the following year.
Overall I think my memories are all happy ones and I hope I live long enough to have many more, especially watching my grandchildren grow up.
